- Mark tests that execute shell commands as Unix-only
- Affects runtime::traits, skills::testing, cron::scheduler tests
- These tests pass on Unix (CI) but fail on Windows (local dev)

Fix Miele default entity IDs to match HA Miele integration

The HA Miele integration uses 'sensor.washing_machine' and
'sensor.tumble_dryer' for status (not '*_status'), and
'sensor.*_program' for program name (not '*_program_name').

Verified against live Home Assistant instance — all 10 entities
now resolve correctly.

commit.message:
feat: stabilize roundtrip matrix tests

Add semantic roundtrip harness/assertions for per-feature and mixed MD<->DOCX flows, fix markdown stringify transforms, preserve custom annotation nodes in builder fallback, and improve OMML n-ary operator parsing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update timeslot logic completely (#30)

* feat: add gender field to registration and profile forms, update toast methods from pending to loading

* fix: add name to gender select element

* feat: integrate react-phone-number-input for phone number fields in HostRegister and Profile

* fix: filter out selected activities from the activity dropdown in HostRegister

* Add required param to PhoneInput

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add phone number input validation in HostRegister

* feat: add confirmation modal on timeslot deletion

* partial update to timeslot logic

* feat: update API calls to fetch experiences and swap timeslot fetching with experience fetching

* feat: create experiences instead of timeslots and add dayOfWeek

* fix: images not being displayed in Timeslot.jsx

* fix: update time slot handling to use correct rule properties

* feat: update timeslot creation and edit timeslot to work with new experiences (mostly)

* fix: activities not showing in edit timeslot unless you refresh page

* feat: update image upload and deletion logic to work with experiences

* feat: styling changes to Profile.jsx and TImeslot.jsx

* refactor: rename modal class for timeslot component

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(changes): show binary file label instead of diff for binary files

- Add isBinary?: boolean to FileChange type
- Add isBinaryBuffer() helper (null-byte scan of first 8 KB) in both
  tool-impls.ts and claude.ts
- implWriteFileWithChange / implDeleteFileWithChange now read files as
  Buffers and emit FileChange { isBinary: true, diff: '' } for binary
  files instead of silently dropping the change
- claude.ts native Write/Edit intercept uses the same Buffer-based
  detection for before/after reads
- ChangesPanel shows 'Binary file — diff not available.' in place of
  DiffView, and suppresses the +N/-N stat badge for binary entries

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix rustfmt formatting for assert macro

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(changes): collapse file entries by default; show new file content for untracked files

- All file entries (session writes + git diff) now start collapsed
- Untracked git diff entries lazy-load the file content on expand via
  ipc.readFile, rendering each line as a green '+' addition
- Line count badge (+N) is derived from the loaded content for untracked files
- Badge label changed from 'untracked' to 'new file' for clarity
- Updated DiffView colouring tests to click-expand before asserting

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(equations): add two-way markdown latex and word omml support

- Add markdown equation plugin for inline $...$ and block ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___$LASTEXITCODE...___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___$LASTEXITCODE nodes
- Wire equation parse/stringify into markdown reader/writer pipelines
- Extend DocxBuilder with OMML math emission via docx math primitives
- Add OoxmlEquationParser with subset-first OMML to LaTeX mapping and warnings
- Extend DocxParser to extract inline/block OMML equations and surface warnings
- Surface equation warnings in pull and diff outputs
- Add unit tests for equation plugin, OMML parser, and DocxParser equation extraction
- Update README and OOXML notes for equation support and limitations

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(changes): show +added/-removed line counts on diff file headers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(changes): parse git diff into per-file collapsible entries

Previously the git diff section rendered the entire diff as one monolithic
block. Now it parses the output into individual file entries that can be
expanded/collapsed independently, matching the session-writes section.

- parseGitDiff() splits on 'diff --git' boundaries and separates the
  untracked-files footer appended by the backend
- GitFileDiff component renders each file with a collapsible header showing
  the relative path and a badge: 'modified' (blue) or 'untracked' (yellow)
- Untracked files show a note instead of a diff (no content available)
- Empty state shows 'No uncommitted changes' instead of blank

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(changes): improve git diff command to show all uncommitted changes

Three issues with the original 'git diff' command:
1. Only showed unstaged changes — staged modifications were invisible
2. Never showed untracked new files written by the agent
3. Electron doesn't inherit shell PATH on macOS, so 'git' could be ENOENT
   even when it is installed

Fixes:
- Use 'git diff HEAD --no-color' to show all tracked-file changes (both
  staged and unstaged) vs the last commit
- Fall back to 'git diff --cached --no-color' when there is no HEAD yet
  (new repo with no commits, detected via 'ambiguous argument' stderr)
- Append untracked files via 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard'
  so brand-new files written by the agent are visible
- Inject /usr/bin:/usr/local/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin into PATH so git is
  found in Electron's restricted environment on macOS

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update checklist_autonomy_default_is_workspace_scoped test for Termux

Termux-first deployment has no workspace restrictions and no
forbidden paths, unlike the original multi-platform secure defaults.

Fixes test: config::schema::tests::checklist_autonomy_default_is_workspace_scoped

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(changes): add tests for ChangesPanel, addFileChange, and SESSION_FILE_CHANGE

- ChangesPanel: DiffView line colouring (green/red/blue/context), empty
  states (no session, no changes, file path shown, write/delete badges,
  no project path fallback)
- useSessionStore: addFileChange accumulation, isolation across sessions,
  tabSwitchRequest / clearTabSwitchRequest
- useSessionEvents: SESSION_FILE_CHANGE listener registration, store
  update, tabSwitchRequest set, multi-change accumulation

Each new describe block resets relevant store slices in beforeEach to
prevent cross-test state bleed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: document allowedTools vs tools SDK footgun in CLAUDE.md and claude.ts

Add a warning comment at the allowedTools usage site and a new 'Known SDK
Footguns' section in CLAUDE.md explaining that:
- allowedTools = auto-approve without prompting (does NOT restrict tools)
- tools = restrict available built-in tools AND blocks MCP tools

This distinction caused a regression where changing allowedTools to tools
silently disabled all MCP tool calls. The doc makes the intent explicit so
the same mistake isn't repeated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(changes): revert to allowedTools and intercept native Write/Edit tool calls

The 'tools' option in the Claude Code SDK restricts ALL tools including MCP
tools, leaving the agent unable to do anything. Revert to 'allowedTools'
which only controls auto-approval without restricting availability.

Instead, track file changes by intercepting native Write/Edit/MultiEdit
tool_use blocks directly in the claude.ts message loop:
- On tool_use: read before-content from disk, store in pendingFileChanges
- On tool_result: read after-content from disk, compute unified diff with
  createPatch, emit SESSION_FILE_CHANGE to the renderer

This covers all file-write paths for the Claude provider (native Write,
Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEditCell). The Copilot provider continues to use
the implWriteFileWithChange wrapper in the tool handler.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use individual pg connection params to bypass URL encoding issues

- buildPgPool() checks DB_HOST/DB_USER/DB_PASS first (no URL encoding)
- Falls back to DATABASE_URL if individual params not set
- Fixes Supabase pgBouncer 'Tenant or user not found' error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[BUZZOK-30024] Revert unintended GH Actions and Taskfile changes (#39)

* Revert base component prediction_environment to per-template with env var injection

Update autonomy_config_default test for Termux defaults

The test was expecting old Supervised defaults but Termux now uses
Full autonomy with no restrictions. Updated assertions to match
the new Termux-first configuration.

Fixes test: config::schema::tests::autonomy_config_default

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace SB_MGMT_TOKEN with direct pg DATABASE_URL connection

- supabase_query now uses pg Pool via DATABASE_URL (no PAT needed)
- supabase_list_tables uses same direct SQL path
- supabase_get_logs falls back gracefully if pg_logs not accessible
- Add pg@^8.13.0 + @types/pg deps
- Remove SB_MGMT_TOKEN dependency entirely

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix always_ask to override Full autonomy level

The approval logic was checking autonomy level before always_ask,
which meant Full autonomy bypassed always_ask entirely. This fixes
the order to check always_ask first, honoring the design intent
that always_ask overrides everything.

Fixes test: approval::tests::always_ask_overrides_auto_approve

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(changes): use tools instead of allowedTools to restrict native Claude tools

allowedTools only auto-approves tools without permission prompts — it does
NOT restrict which tools are available. The correct field is 'tools', which
acts as an explicit allowlist of native built-in tools.

With allowedTools, Claude was using its native Write/Edit tools directly,
bypassing our MCP write_file wrapper and never emitting SESSION_FILE_CHANGE
events. Switching to tools: [...] forces Claude to use our MCP tools for
file writes, which correctly capture diffs and emit change events.

Also fix the duplicate SESSION_TOOL_CALL for MCP tool calls (previously
emitted by both claude.ts and mcp-tools.ts), and strip the verbose
'mcp__aichemist-tools__' prefix from tool names in traces and tool call
events so they display as 'write_file' rather than
'mcp__aichemist-tools__write_file'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(changes): add Changes panel with session diffs and git diff

- Add FileChange + SessionFileChangeEvent types to src/types/index.ts
- Add SESSION_FILE_CHANGE + GET_GIT_DIFF IPC channels
- Add GET_GIT_DIFF handler in main.ts (git diff via execSync)
- Add implWriteFileWithChange + implDeleteFileWithChange helpers in
  tool-impls.ts using the 'diff' package (createPatch)
- Wrap write_file + delete_file in mcp-tools.ts and copilot.ts to
  capture before/after and emit SESSION_FILE_CHANGE events
- Add sessionFileChanges state + addFileChange action to useSessionStore
- Add tabSwitchRequest + requestTabSwitch + clearTabSwitchRequest to
  useSessionStore for cross-component tab navigation
- Handle SESSION_FILE_CHANGE in useSessionEvents; auto-switches panel
- Wire tabSwitchRequest in WorkspaceView via useEffect
- Build ChangesPanel.tsx: collapsible per-file diffs with syntax
  colouring (+/-/@@) + git diff section with Refresh button
- Add 'changes' to ContextTab union, ContextPanel render, and ToolStrip
- Add getGitDiff to electronAPI mock to satisfy typecheck

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(notes): add changes panel spec (session writes + git diff)

commit.message:
[bug] Replace context.TODO() with propagated context.Context (#156)

Thread context.Context through all AWS SDK API call chains instead of
using context.TODO(). This enables proper cancellation and timeout
support throughout the codebase.

Changes:
- Add ctx context.Context as first parameter to all functions making
  AWS API calls in lib/ and config/ packages
- Replace all context.TODO() with ctx in those functions
- Update all cmd/ command handlers to create context.Background() and
  pass it through the call chain
- Update all test files to pass context.Background() to modified
  functions

The existing tgw_routetables.go already had the correct pattern and
was not modified. No per-call timeouts were added; context is simply
threaded through for future cancellation/timeout support.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: fog
repository.description: Tool for managing your CloudFormation

commit.message:
fix: abort deployment when prechecks fail with stop-on-failed-prechecks enabled (#155)

runPrechecks previously returned only a display string and used the
stop-on-failed-prechecks config solely to choose between two message
variants. deployTemplate had no way to act on the precheck outcome and
unconditionally proceeded to create and deploy the changeset.

Change runPrechecks to return (string, bool) where the bool signals
abort. deployTemplate now checks this signal and exits before changeset
creation when prechecks fail and stop-on-failed-prechecks is true.

Also enhance integration tests to verify changeset creation is actually
skipped when prechecks signal abort.

Fixes T-567

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add GitHub Pages deployment via GitHub Actions

Fix APNs channel creation and unguarded JSON parse crashes

APNs channel creation:
- Fix request body to use correct Apple API fields (message-storage-policy, push-type)
- Read channel ID from apns-channel-id response header instead of body
- Return response headers from makeChannelRequest()
- Remove incorrect apns-push-type/apns-topic headers from channel management requests
- Fix listRemoteChannels() to use /all-channels endpoint
- Remove broken name-matching sync logic (Apple channels have no names)

JSON parse crashes:
- Add response.ok checks to fetchSchedule() and fetchRhizomePhotos() in server.ts
- Add .catch() handlers to prevent unhandled promise rejections
- Add response.ok checks to Miele getToken(), refreshToken(), getActivePrograms()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add .vite to .gitignore

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix T-590: Fix Report Frontmatter Selecting Random/Incorrect Event Metadata (#154)

* T-590: Fix report frontmatter selecting random/incorrect event metadata

generateFrontMatter iterated directly over a stacks map and all events,
overwriting the same result keys each pass. Go map iteration order is
non-deterministic, so the final frontmatter reflected whichever event
happened to be visited last — producing different output between runs.
The function also ignored reportFlags.LatestOnly, so frontmatter could
describe an event not shown in the report body.

Fix:
- Sort stacks by key before iteration (deterministic, matching report body)
- Select the single newest event (by StartDate) across all stacks
- When LatestOnly is set, only consider the latest event per stack
- Populate frontmatter once from the selected event
- Sort keys in buildSimpleHTMLTable for deterministic HTML summary output

Add 5 regression tests covering determinism, latest-event selection,
LatestOnly flag respect, multi-stack scenarios, and empty input.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review: fix test parallelism, update docs

- Remove t.Parallel() from frontmatter tests that mutate global state
  (settings, reportFlags, viper) to prevent data races
- Add comment clarifying GetEvents returns events oldest-first
- Update --frontmatter flag description to reflect multi-stack support

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix GetCfnStacks map keys: use stack name instead of stack ID (#153)

GetCfnStacks was keying its returned map by StackId (an AWS ARN) instead
of StackName. All callers (dependencies, report) treat map keys as
human-readable stack names for filtering, sorting, and display, causing
stack name filtering to silently fail.

Changes:
- Change map key from *stack.StackId to *stack.StackName (lib/stacks.go:260)
- Refactor GetCfnStacks to accept CFNExportsAPI interface instead of
  concrete *cloudformation.Client, following the pattern in GetExports,
  to enable unit testing with MockCFNClient
- Add regression tests in lib/getcfnstacks_test.go verifying map keys
  are stack names, key-name consistency, and glob/exact filtering

Fixes T-545

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[bug] Fix T-589: Guard Drift Tag Diff Parsing Against Malformed Property Paths (#152)

* T-589: Guard drift tag diff parsing against malformed property paths

Add nil pointer and bounds checks to tagDifferences in cmd/drift.go:
- Return early when PropertyPath is nil or empty
- Validate pathsplit length before indexing segments
- Use aws.ToString() instead of direct pointer dereference for
  ExpectedValue/ActualValue comparison in the default case
- Guard json.Indent with json.Valid to handle empty strings from nil
- Check buffer length before indexing into expected/actual buffers

Add 7 regression tests covering nil pointers, short paths, and empty
strings to prevent future panics from malformed AWS responses.

- Remove stale line number references from test comments

- Add shouldTagBeHandled behavior note in short_path test

commit.message:
T-602: Fix interactive failed-changeset delete confirmation (#151)

The interactive branch in createChangeset called askForConfirmation()
without capturing its return value, so deleteChangesetConfirmation
always stayed false and deleteChangeset was never invoked when the
user confirmed deletion.

Extract the cleanup logic into a testable handleFailedChangeset helper
that correctly assigns the confirmation result. Uses askForConfirmationFunc
and deleteChangesetFunc for consistency with existing mock patterns.

Add regression test covering interactive confirm, interactive decline,
and non-interactive auto-delete scenarios.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[bug] Fix T-611: Block Unsafe Precheck Commands When Invoked via Path (#149)

* Fix T-611: Block unsafe precheck commands when invoked via path

RunPrechecks only checked exact command names against the denylist,
so path-prefixed invocations like /bin/rm or ./rm bypassed the
safety guard entirely.

Fix: normalise the command with filepath.Base() before the denylist
check so that the base executable name is always compared regardless
of path prefix.

Add regression test TestRunPrechecksUnsafeCommandWithPath covering
absolute paths, relative paths, and bare command names.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review feedback

- Use harmless args (--help) in test cases instead of destructive ones
  (rm -rf /, kill -9 1) to avoid damage if the denylist guard regresses.
- Replace removed TODO with a NOTE acknowledging the denylist is minimal
  and pointing to the bugfix report for the allowlist alternative.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use case-insensitive denylist comparison for precheck commands

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[bug] Fix T-577: Treat precheck execution errors as failed prechecks (#146)

* T-577: Treat precheck execution errors as failed prechecks

When lib.RunPrechecks returned an error (e.g. command not found, unsafe
command, unbalanced quotes), the runPrechecks helper only formatted the
error message without setting PrechecksFailed or updating the deployment
log. This meant the stop-on-failed-prechecks flag was never evaluated
and deployment always proceeded to changeset creation.

Fix:
- Set info.PrechecksFailed and logObj.PreChecks on the error path in
  runPrechecks, matching the existing runtime-failure path
- Add a gate in deployTemplate to exit before changeset creation when
  prechecks failed and stop-on-failed-prechecks is true
- Add regression tests for missing command, unsafe command, and stop
  flag honoring

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Persist deployment log before exit on failed prechecks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
T-612: Fix escaped space handling in splitShellArgs (#144)

splitShellArgs now handles backslash-escaped spaces outside quotes,
matching standard shell behaviour. Previously, input like
'cfn-lint -t path\ with\ spaces/template.yaml' was incorrectly split
at each escaped space.

Added a case for backslash escapes outside single/double quotes that
consumes the next character literally. Includes 6 new regression tests
covering escaped spaces, mixed quoting styles, and edge cases.

feat: unsaved changes indicator in explorer tree

- Gold dot appears next to files in the explorer that have unsaved changes

commit.message:
feat: clickable breadcrumb path segments

- Replace single TextBlock with ItemsControl of clickable Button segments
- Clicking a folder segment sets it as workspace root
- Clicking the file segment opens/focuses that file
- Directory segments show in muted color, file segment highlighted
- Separator '' rendered between segments

commit.message:
feat: scrollbar annotation strip (search, errors, bookmarks, caret)

- Add thin Canvas overlay on right edge of editor area
- Orange marks for search matches, red for errors, yellow for warnings
- Blue marks for bookmarks, green marker for current caret position
- Refreshed on search, diagnostics update, and heavy visual refresh
- Non-interactive overlay (IsHitTestVisible=False)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Toggle via View > Relative Line Numbers menu or Settings checkbox
- Updates on every caret movement when enabled
- Setting persisted in AppState across sessions

feat: add Docker multi-stage builds and GitHub Actions CI pipeline

- Dockerfile.server: .NET 10 multi-stage, non-root user, healthcheck
- Dockerfile.client: Node 22 + nginx:1.27-alpine, SPA routing
- nginx.conf: SPA fallback + static asset caching
- .github/workflows/ci.yml: parallel backend test + image builds
- .dockerignore: exclude dev artifacts

TODO: wire postgres service container for integration tests
TODO: add Artifact Registry push when GCP is configured

- Replace plain text buttons with card-style items showing file icon,

- Add FileNameConverter and DirectoryNameConverter helper classes
- Tooltips show full path on hover
